questions.text: In which year did Mahatma Gandhi return to India from South Africa?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi returned to India in January 1915 after successfully leading a non-violent protest in South Africa.

questions.text: The Rowlatt Act of 1919 allowed the government to:
questions.explanation: The Rowlatt Act gave the British government enormous powers to repress political activities and allowed detention without trial for up to two years.

questions.text: Who was the author of the book 'Hind Swaraj'?
questions.explanation: In 'Hind Swaraj' (1909), Mahatma Gandhi declared that British rule was established in India with the cooperation of Indians.

questions.text: What led to the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1922?
questions.explanation: Gandhiji called off the movement after a violent incident at Chauri Chaura where a police station was set on fire by protesters.

questions.text: Which movement was started by the 'Dandi March'?
questions.explanation: The Civil Disobedience Movement began in 1930 when Gandhiji reached Dandi and ceremonially violated the salt law.

questions.text: The concept of 'Satyagraha' emphasized the power of:
options: Truth and the need to search for truth
questions.explanation: It suggested that if the cause was true, physical force was not necessary to fight the oppressor.

questions.text: Where was the first Satyagraha movement launched by Gandhi in India?
questions.explanation: In 1917, he traveled to Champaran in Bihar to inspire peasants to struggle against the oppressive plantation system.

questions.text: On which day did the Jallianwala Bagh massacre take place?
questions.explanation: General Dyer opened fire on a peaceful crowd gathered in the enclosed ground at Amritsar.

questions.text: The Khilafat Committee was formed in Bombay in 1919 to:
options: Defend the temporal powers of the Ottoman Caliph
questions.explanation: The committee was led by the Ali brothers, Muhammad Ali and Shaukat Ali.

questions.text: At which Congress session was the Non-Cooperation program adopted?
questions.explanation: After much debate, the program was finalized at the Nagpur session in December 1920.

questions.text: Who led the peasant movement in Awadh during the Non-Cooperation Movement?
questions.explanation: Baba Ramchandra was a sanyasi who had earlier been to Fiji as an indentured laborer.

questions.text: Alluri Sitaram Raju was associated with the tribal movement in:
questions.explanation: He claimed to have special powers and led a militant guerrilla movement.

questions.text: Under the Inland Emigration Act of 1859, plantation workers were not permitted to:
options: Leave the tea gardens without permission
questions.explanation: Workers were confined to the gardens and rarely given permission to go home.

questions.text: The Swaraj Party was formed by:
options: Jawaharlal Nehru and Subhash Chandra Bose
questions.explanation: They wanted to enter the provincial councils to oppose British policies from within.

questions.text: The Simon Commission was boycotted in India because:
options: It did not have any Indian member
It was led by a conservative
questions.explanation: The all-British commission was sent to review India's constitutional system, causing widespread outrage.

questions.text: At which session did the Congress formalize the demand for 'Purna Swaraj' (Full Independence)?
questions.explanation: The session was presided over by Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.text: How many miles did Gandhi walk during the Salt March?
questions.explanation: He walked from Sabarmati ashram to Dandi over 24 days.

questions.text: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact was signed in:
questions.explanation: Gandhi agreed to participate in the Second Round Table Conference in London.

questions.text: Who organized the Dalits into the 'Depressed Classes Association' in 1930?
questions.explanation: Ambedkar clashed with Gandhi at the Second Round Table Conference over separate electorates.

questions.text: The Poona Pact of September 1932 was an agreement between:
questions.explanation: It gave reserved seats in provincial and central legislative councils to the Depressed Classes.

questions.text: Who created the first image of 'Bharat Mata'?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay wrote 'Vande Mataram' and created the imagery of the motherland.

questions.text: The famous painting of Bharat Mata as an ascetic figure was painted by:
questions.explanation: She is shown as calm, composed, divine, and spiritual.

questions.text: Who published a massive four-volume collection of Tamil folk tales, 'The Folklore of Southern India'?
questions.explanation: He believed that folklore was national literature and the most trustworthy manifestation of people's thoughts.

questions.text: The spinning wheel in the center of the Swaraj flag represented:
questions.explanation: The flag was designed by Gandhi in 1921.

questions.text: Who was known as 'Frontier Gandhi'?
questions.explanation: He was a devout disciple of Gandhi and led the movement in Peshawar.

questions.text: The FICCI (Federation of Indian Chamber of Commerce and Industries) was formed in:
questions.explanation: It was established by prominent industrialists like G.D. Birla and Purshottamdas Thakurdas.

questions.text: Which movement was launched in 1942 with the slogan 'Do or Die'?
questions.explanation: It was a massive mass struggle launched during the Second World War.

questions.text: The 'Simon Commission' arrived in India in:
questions.explanation: It was greeted with the slogan 'Simon Go Back'.

questions.text: Vande Mataram was included in which novel?
questions.explanation: The novel was written by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay.

questions.text: What was the significance of 26 January 1930?
options: It was celebrated as Independence Day
questions.explanation: It was the date fixed by the Lahore Congress to celebrate Independence Day every year.

questions.text: The first World War created a new political and economic situation by:
questions.explanation: These factors led to extreme hardship for the common people in India.

questions.text: The 'Oudh Kisan Sabha' was set up by:
questions.explanation: Within a month, over 300 branches had been set up in the villages around the region.

questions.text: Who presided over the 1929 Lahore session of Congress?
questions.explanation: Under his leadership, the goal of Full Independence was declared.

questions.text: The feeling of nationalism was spread by:
questions.explanation: Cultural processes played a vital role in capturing people's imagination.

questions.text: Inland Emigration Act of 1859 prevented workers from leaving tea gardens in which state?
questions.explanation: Plantation workers in Assam had their own understanding of Gandhi and the notion of Swaraj.

questions.text: The 'Swaraj Flag' designed by Gandhi had how many colors?
questions.explanation: It was a tricolour (red, green, and white) with a spinning wheel in the center.

questions.text: Which movement was started to support the temporal powers of the Khalifa?
questions.explanation: Gandhi saw this as an opportunity to bring Muslims and Hindus under the umbrella of a unified national movement.

questions.text: The Salt Satyagraha began from which place?
questions.explanation: The march started from his ashram at Sabarmati to the coastal town of Dandi.

questions.text: Who is the author of the novel 'Anandamath'?
questions.explanation: This novel contains the hymn 'Vande Mataram' which became a slogan for nationalists.

questions.text: The demand for separate electorates for Muslims was strongly put forward by:
questions.explanation: This was a major point of difference between the Congress and the Muslim League.
